description_long: This is a heavily altered hardcover book with its pages intricately cut and sculpted to form a three-dimensional scene. The open pages reveal a diorama featuring a human figure, a house, and an eye, all emerging from the carved paper. Thick, twisted white ropes snake through the book, weaving in and out of the pages and adding a tactile, sculptural element. The book's cover is a muted beige with a yellow spine, and there is a crest and a handwritten label on the back. The edges of the pages are uneven and appear aged, with some discoloration and curling. The overall appearance is that of a mixed-media art piece, blending literature and sculpture.
description: An altered hardcover book with sculpted pages, rope elements, and a diorama scene cut into the open pages. The cover is beige with a yellow spine and features a crest and handwritten label.
description_view_0: The open book displays intricately cut pages forming a diorama with a human figure, house, eye, and thick white ropes woven through the pages.
description_view_1: A side view showing the book's thickness, the curling and uneven edges of the pages, and the protruding ropes.
description_view_2: The back cover and spine are visible, with a yellow spine, a crest, and a handwritten label; the ropes and sculpted page edges are also apparent.
description_view_3: An angled view of the back cover, highlighting the crest, label, and the way the ropes emerge from the book's interior.
attribute_cover_illustration: No illustration visible. The front cover is plain, with no artwork or imagery, only text and a crest on the back.
attribute_title_text: FROM THE EARTH TO THE MOON
attribute_spine_design: The spine is yellow with a maroon vertical label containing the title in gold text. There are thin decorative lines above and below the label, and the text is oriented vertically.
attribute_publisher_logo: A circular crest with laurel leaves and a central emblem is visible on the back cover, likely representing the publisher's logo.
